task.name: Douyin Video Download
task.goal: Open a Douyin share link in a real browser, reuse the browser-generated signed detail request plus cookies, extract a playable MP4 URL from the detail JSON, and download the video artifact.

expected_outputs.video_path: Downloaded MP4 file stored in the task artifacts directory
expected_outputs.detail_path: JSON copy of the aweme detail payload used to derive the video URL
expected_outputs.summary_path: JSON summary with resolved URL, cookie count, selected video source, and download bytes

environment.entry_url: Short share URL or canonical /video/<aweme_id> URL provided at runtime
environment.profile_expectation: Task must run inside browser-cli so the page can mint signed detail URLs and Douyin cookies before Python requests replays the detail request.
browser_assumptions: The short share link redirects to a canonical /video/<aweme_id> page.
The canonical page emits an aweme detail request that appears in performance resource entries.
The browser profile accumulates enough Douyin cookies for the signed detail URL to return JSON instead of an empty 200 response.

steps: Open the Douyin URL with browser-cli and poll page state until a signed aweme detail request URL appears in performance resource entries.
Read all browser cookies through browser-cli and replay the signed detail request with requests.
Parse aweme_detail.video.play_addr first, then JSON-based fallbacks, choose the first playable MP4 URL, and download it with a Douyin Referer.
key_assertions: The task should not depend on manual selector clicks or direct Playwright.
detail.json must decode as JSON and contain aweme_detail.video.
The selected media URL must download to a non-empty local MP4 artifact.

retryable_steps: Increase wait_rounds or wait_seconds if the page is slow to expose the aweme detail resource entry.
Rerun the task if the signed media URL expires before the download starts.
alternate_paths: If play_addr is absent, the task falls back to play_addr_h264, play_addr_265, and bit_rate[*].play_addr from the same detail JSON.
recovery_hints.stale_ref_strategy: No semantic refs are required; the critical wait is page state becoming ready enough to expose the signed detail resource entry.
known_wait_points: Redirect from the short share URL to the canonical /video/<aweme_id> page.
Hydration completing far enough that performance resource entries include the aweme detail request.
anti_bot_recovery: Do not reuse only document.cookie. The signed detail request returned HTTP 200 with an empty body until the full browser-cli cookie jar was replayed.
Keep Referer and User-Agent aligned with the real Douyin page when replaying the detail request and downloading the MP4.

failures.symptom: The aweme detail request returns HTTP 200 with Content-Length: 0 and x-whale-throughput-abort-data when replayed without the full browser cookie jar.
failures.lesson: browser-cli cookies is required for this site; document.cookie alone is insufficient.

failures.symptom: browser-cli network capture exposes request metadata only, not response bodies.
failures.lesson: For Douyin detail extraction, performance resource entries plus requests replay currently work more reliably than trying to read the detail JSON directly from browser-cli network capture.

semantic_ref_notes: This task is primarily browser-state driven rather than ref-driven.
knowledge.lazy_load_pattern: Hydration/resource discovery rather than infinite scroll
anti_bot_notes: Douyin signs detail requests in-browser and couples successful replay to the active browser cookie jar.
The current browser-cli network capture layer records fetch/XHR request metadata but not response bodies.
output_interpretation_notes: detail.json preserves the exact JSON used for media URL derivation.
download-summary.json shows which JSON field produced the chosen video URL.
